content: Imagine you have a big notebook that lots of people share, where everyone writes down every time someone trades a toy with someone else. This notebook is special because it helps keep track of who has which toys, so everyone can agree on who owns what. The really cool thing about this notebook is that lots of people have a copy of it, so if someone tries to cheat and say they have a toy they don't really have, the other notebooks can say "no, that's not true!" When someone makes a new trade, like swapping a toy car for a toy doll, the update gets sent to all the notebooks, and then everyone agrees that the trade happened. It's like a big team effort, where lots of people work together to make sure the notebooks are all the same and accurate. This system is called blockchain, and it's used by grown-ups to keep track of money and trades, without needing a central bank. Just like our toy-trading notebook, blockchain is a team effort that keeps everything fair and honest.
